column1: In-app engineering notes captured during design: things the app (SHOAPPNATIVE) must do that are not pure backend rules. Each note points to the spec sheet it depends on. These fold into the Build Backlog.

column4: The chosen gym days must be editable after onboarding, and a single week must be reschedulable. A user set to Mon/Tue/Thu should be able to move Monday to Friday from week 2, or shift a session when life gets in the way.
column5: Build an editable training-days setting. On change, re-run Schedule Rules to re-place the week (rest floor, 48h spacing, commitment adjacency) without wiping progression_state. Add a per-session 'move' action that reschedules one session and folds it forward, distinct from a 'miss'.

column4: When a user misses a day, they should be able to catch up rather than lose the session. The backend spec (SCH07/08) already shifts or folds; the app needs the UI for it.
column5: Surface a 'catch up' / 'do this next session' option when a planned session was missed. Implement the SCH07 shift-forward and slot-fold logic, and the SCH08 3-week re-plan-and-suggest. A planned_absence (Exam Protocol) suppresses the penalty entirely (SCH10).
column6: Schedule Rules SCH07/08/10, Exam Survival Protocol

column4: The outside-activity answers must reach the workout generator, not just sit in the profile. Right now the spec wires them to External Commitments; the app must actually read them at generation time.
column5: At Generator Flow step 4, read users.commitments and apply the External Commitments row (volume cuts, adjacency blocks, interval-slot removal, next-day RIR). Re-apply whenever the user edits their activities.
column6: External Commitments, Generator Flow, Onboarding Questions step 8

column2: Activity step is optional & editable later
column4: The 'tell us about your activities' step must be skippable at onboarding and fully editable in-app afterwards, so a user can add a sport mid-program and have it factored in.
column5: Make the activity step skippable (no blocking validation). Provide an in-app activities editor. On any change, trigger CC03 re-application so the program adapts.

column2: Injury region tagging is enough (no free text)
column4: Injury free text was removed. The app must rely on region chips mapping to Injury Modifications rows. Confirm every chip maps to a region that has a rule, and that Achilles/calf routes to the ankle rule.
column5: Map each chip to a region token at capture time. Ensure the region set on the chips is a superset of the Injury Modifications regions. Anything not covered by a chip must go through the safety check, not a training text box.

column4: The free-text 'anything else we should know' box must be scanned for health red-flags before it is treated as harmless, otherwise a serious disclosure gets stored and ignored.
column5: Run the free text through a red-flag check (cardiac, neurological, pregnancy, medical-advice language). On a hit, route the user to the screening/clearance path (S05) rather than generating. Log the disclosure.
column6: Coach AI Operating Rules, Screening Outcomes, Safety Rules S05
